title: Pancratium skinnerianum
taxonomy: Pancratium skinnerianum belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features. Pancratium skinnerianum is a species of bulbous perennial plant, native to the Mediterranean region. It is a member of the genus Pancratium, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The classification of Pancratium skinnerianum is further supported by its growth habits and flower characteristics.

intro: Pancratium skinnerianum, also known as Skinner's pancratium, is a bulbous perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which provide relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Pancratium skinnerianum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used to make cough syrups and expectorant preparations.
